title: FastAPI project structure and dependencies setup
description: Establish the complete FastAPI project structure with all required dependencies, environment variable configuration, and update docker-compose.yaml to use SQLite instead of Postgres.
acceptanceCriteria: requirements.txt includes all needed packages: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, pydantic, python-jose[cryptography], passlib[bcrypt], python-dotenv
Project is organized into logical modules: models/, routers/, schemas/, dependencies/
docker-compose.yaml no longer references Postgres — only api and web services
SQLite data directory is mounted as a Docker volume for persistence
App starts successfully with docker-compose up

steps.details: Update src/api/requirements.txt to include: fastapi==0.115.x, uvicorn==0.32.x, sqlalchemy==2.0.x, pydantic==2.x, python-jose[cryptography]==3.3.x, passlib[bcrypt]==1.7.x, python-dotenv==1.0.x. Pin to compatible versions.

Create the following directories and __init__.py files in src/api/: models/ (for SQLAlchemy models), routers/ (for FastAPI route handlers), schemas/ (for Pydantic request/response schemas), dependencies/ (for FastAPI Depends functions like get_db, get_current_user).

steps.description: Update docker-compose.yaml to remove Postgres and add SQLite volume
steps.details: In src/docker-compose.yaml: remove the 'db' service entirely, remove the postgres_data volume, remove depends_on db from api service, remove DATABASE_URL env var from api service (or update to sqlite path), add a named volume 'sqlite_data' mapped to /app/data in the api service. Add environment variable DATABASE_URL=sqlite:////app/data/db.sqlite to the api service.

In src/api/Dockerfile, ensure the /app/data directory is created with RUN mkdir -p /app/data. Verify the CMD starts uvicorn correctly: CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]. Check the Dockerfile exists and works.

steps.description: Verify the app starts with docker-compose
steps.details: Run 'docker-compose up --build' in the src/ directory. Verify: no Postgres connection errors, API container starts and logs 'Application startup complete', GET http://localhost:8000/health returns {"status": "ok"}.

technicalNotes: SQLite database path uses 4 slashes for absolute path: sqlite:////app/data/db.sqlite
The api Dockerfile must create /app/data or the database file creation will fail
docker-compose volumes persist between container restarts — the SQLite file won't be lost on restart
